Supplier description:
CST Fraud prevention, 2FA, SMS.
2. High-level requirements
Wise are looking to enhance their fraud and authentication capabilities across their digital channels.
2.1. Wise requirements
There are two core requirements:
2.1.1. Enhance Security and Fraud Prevention
Wise are looking to
• Protect against use of compromised credentials and prevent account takeover
• Identify threats such as a malware, RAT, and BOTs
2.1.2. Improve User Experience
Wise would like to improve the user experience by replacing active authenticators such as SMS OTP with passive authentication:
• Reduce/increase friction in authentication process through risk-based policies
• Passive authentication capabilities, such as behavioural biometrics and device fingerprinting
2.2. Callsign alignment to requirements
Wise’s requirements are met using the following Callsign capabilities:
2.2.1. Device Identification
• Tracking of device identifiers across multiple transactions of the same device
• Detect when the same device is used by multiple accounts
• Detect when one account is accessed by multiple devices.
• Detect when a device has previously committed fraud
Cryptographically bind a browser against a user to use as a possession authentication factor
2.2.2. Location Intelligence
• Accurately detect location of users based on geolocation data
• Provide fallback capabilities to location intelligence based on IP address and network information
2.2.3. Behavioural Authentication
• Build an individual behavioural model for each user and perform a 1:1 comparison against their model for each interaction
• Detect deviations in a user’s physical interaction with the GUI (e.g. because of a user’s credentials being compromised/socially engineered and used by a different user or BOT).
o With the current Wise login journey there is a known limitation that the user only has to type in their password when they are remembered on the device. When a user performs the forgotten password journey they will only type in their email address. Callsign’s behavioural biometrics capability is most effective when it assesses a consistent memorable string (e.g. email address) rather than different strings (e.g. OTPs).
2.2.4. Behavioural Analytics
• Assess the user’s behaviour against global threat models to detect specific indicators that relate to known fraud Modus Operandi.
• Detect Remote Access Takeover of web sessions
• Detect BOTs that are performing credential stuffing and fraudulent password reset attempts

EY currently provides Wise with advice for all matters relating to Tax. EY has also supported the FATCA & CRS reporting obligations we have in place for UK and SG Assets product customers by producing reporting submission files.

EY FIRST is a tool offered by EY which facilitates the generation of reportable files. The tool validates reporting data against country-specific requirements and produces the required tax authority submission file. It also monitors changes to the requirements for each country and provides management reporting and data analysis functions.

We plan to utilise EY FIRST for all of our FATCA & CRS reporting obligations globally. We will be looking to use a licence model of deployment for EY FIRST which means that Wise will manage the process and generate reports using EY FIRST. This means that we can control the timings of when we need reports, use the tool for real-time validation of data and also use the tool for ad hoc testing of data for audit purposes. The tool also contains built in management reporting to help us oversee the end-to-end reporting process.

EY FIRST can also be used for other regulatory reporting so we will also be using this to complete quarterly CESOP reporting.

Supplier description:
Wise is looking to set up an evaluative user testing program to 
test new designs for their account solution. The program will 
consist of six rounds running every two to four weeks. The 
markets are initially the UK and a variety of EU countries, while 
the US and Brazil might be added later on.

Loop UX will handle all logistics and serve as your 
extended team throughout the program life cycle (placing external researcher in the Account space). They will handle 
all services related to recruitment and we make sure that the 
setup supports live streaming for all remote sessions. 
They will provide a dedicated moderator and a small research 
team to handle this program in order to stay informed and 
build knowledge on the topic. This enables them to provide 
added support and flexibility throughout the program.
For each round, they will deliver video recordings and a 
topline report following a Wise template. Other deliverables 
are described in this proposal and can be delivered at 
additional cost.
Each round of research will be based on recruiting six participants 
and two backups for 60 min remote, in-depth interviews. LOI 
and sample size can be adjusted as requested.
The proposal is based on running all research rounds in 
English. The cost covers rounds in the UK as well as rounds 
in EU markets with a high English proficiency. 

If other markets are requested, Loop UX will talk to Wise on 
a case by case basis on the feasibility of running sessions 
in English and how this will affect the recruitment cost. If 
Wise wish to conduct research in markets in their native 
language, Loop UX can provide both local moderators and 
simultaneous interpreters at additional cost. 

Scope of work:
● Project management of all logistics from start to finish
● Recruitment of 6 participants and 2 backups including incentives handling 
and making sure everyone signs NDA & consent form for each round - 
Wise users recruited via lists and non-Wise users recruited via databases
● Remote tech setup and live streaming of all sessions
● Research preparation incl. collaborative question guide creation and 
question guide Q&A between client and moderators
● Moderation of sessions incl. daily debriefs during research days
● Analysis and reporting 
● Screener creation 
● Video Recordings
● Topline report for each round incl. report readout (additional)

Additional deliverables: 
● Takeaway sheet (summary of findings) for each round
● Data spreadsheet for each round
● Transcriptions (digitally made, manually checked)
● VIdeo highlight reel for each round

We are a private sector, not-for-profit company limited by guarantee. Our work is funded by our members, but our approach is sector-neutral.  Our small executive team is drawn from a range of backgrounds.

We also work closely with the government, but our ambition is to help our members protect their customers well ahead of any legislative and regulatory changes.

Address (Street Line 1): 1 Angel Lane, London, EC4r 3AB
Supplier description:
Wise is facing significant challenges with Authorised Push Payment (APP) fraud due to incomplete transaction data sharing between financial institutions. With the impending APP Scam Reimbursement regulations effective October 7, 2024, Wise must enhance its fraud detection capabilities to mitigate financial losses and reputational damage. The proposed solution is to integrate with Mastercard Vocalink, a third-party provider that facilitates data sharing across the UK financial sector, providing a comprehensive view of transactions. 

This integration will employ three key tools:

Mule Insights Tactical Solution (MITS) - Detects and alerts institutions about high-risk transactional activity. 

Consumer Fraud Risk (CFR) - Provides real-time pre-transaction risk scores to prevent fraud. 

Inbound Risk Notification (IRN) - Alerts Wise about high-risk incoming transactions for proactive measures.

By leveraging these tools, Wise aims to reduce both inbound and outbound APP scam volumes, enhance fraud prevention, and comply with new regulatory requirements. The commercial terms with Mastercard Vocalink are favorable, allowing a cost-free integration period until April 3, 2025. This strategic move is expected to result in significant fraud reduction, improved customer protection, and long-term financial and reputational benefits for Wise.

More details https://transferwise.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CAA/pages/3377040455/Mastercard+Vocalink+Integration+Consumer+Fraud+Risk

Supplier description:
Yugabyte is a database technology company that provides an open-source distributed SQL database called YugabyteDB. The Database Engineering team at Wise would like to conduct a POC of YugabyteDB to validate whether it can address limitations of our current PostgreSQL and MariaDB (deployed on AWS RDS) estate relating to scalability, availability, multi-region and multi-cloud deployments. 

YugabyteDB delivers the capabilities of advanced databases like PostgreSQL and the scalability and resilience of modern cloud-native architectures. It is engineered to handle large-scale, high-performance applications by offering automatic sharding and horizontal scalability, which address the challenges of scaling PostgreSQL and MariaDB on RDS (Relational Database Service). This architecture also addresses the high availability and fault tolerance limitations of PostgreSQL and MariaDB on RDS by providing automatic replication and fast, consistent failover. Additionally, while PostgreSQL and MariaDB in RDS require explicit configuration and management to support multi-region deployments, YugabyteDB inherently supports global data distribution, reducing latency for global applications.
